title: Expired SSL Certificate on Billing Service
description: Users are unable to access the billing service. The issue affects multiple teams and is causing business impact. A cascading failure may be involved. Diagnose and fully resolve.

ticket.subject: [CRITICAL] Billing service down — customers cannot pay — immediate action required
ticket.body: Our billing service at billing.company.com is completely inaccessible. Customers are getting SSL errors and our internal teams cannot process payments. We have at least 3 enterprise clients unable to complete transactions. This started approximately 40 minutes ago. Sales team is furious. Fix this NOW. I need a full RCA when it's resolved.

logs: [2024-02-03 11:00:03] CERTBOT: Certificate for billing.company.com EXPIRED at 11:00:00 UTC
[2024-02-03 11:00:03] CERTBOT: Auto-renewal is DISABLED — manual renewal required
[2024-02-03 11:00:05] KONG: SSL handshake failure for billing.company.com — certificate expired
[2024-02-03 11:00:10] BILLING-SVC: Upstream requests failing — SSL termination broken at gateway
[2024-02-03 11:02:15] NGINX: Billing upstream SSL error — returning 502 to clients
[2024-02-03 11:42:00] MONITOR: 42 minutes of continuous SSL failure on billing.company.com

observations.check_logs:
SYSTEM LOGS (last 60 min):
[2024-02-03 11:00:03] CERTBOT: Certificate for billing.company.com EXPIRED at 11:00:00 UTC
[2024-02-03 11:00:03] CERTBOT: Auto-renewal is DISABLED — manual renewal required
[2024-02-03 11:00:05] KONG: SSL handshake failure for billing.company.com — certificate expired
[2024-02-03 11:00:05] KONG: Gateway entering UNHEALTHY state
[2024-02-03 11:02:15] NGINX: Billing upstream SSL error — returning 502 to clients

Pattern: SSL failure cascade starting at 11:00:00 UTC.
observations.check_service_status:
SERVICE STATUS OVERVIEW:
  billing-service.service:  ACTIVE (running) — port 8443
  kong-gateway.service:     ACTIVE (running) — status: UNHEALTHY
  nginx.service:            ACTIVE (running)

  External reachability:
    billing.company.com:443  — SSL_ERROR_RX_RECORD_TOO_LONG
    billing.company.com:8443 — reachable internally (no SSL at app layer)

  Note: Service is running but gateway SSL termination is broken.
observations.inspect_ssl_certificate:
SSL CERTIFICATE INSPECTION — billing.company.com:
  Subject:     CN=billing.company.com
  Issuer:      Let's Encrypt Authority X3
  Valid From:  2023-11-05 11:00:00 UTC
  Valid Until: 2024-02-03 11:00:00 UTC
  Status:      EXPIRED (expired 42 minutes ago)
  Auto-renew:  DISABLED
  Days expired: 0 (expired today at 11:00 UTC)

  Browser error: NET::ERR_CERT_DATE_INVALID
  Recommendation: Renew certificate immediately.
observations.identify_cert_expiry:
CERTIFICATE EXPIRY ANALYSIS:
  Root Cause Confirmed: SSL certificate for billing.company.com expired at 2024-02-03 11:00:00 UTC.
  Auto-renewal was disabled — likely disabled during a maintenance window and never re-enabled.
  The expired cert caused Kong gateway to enter unhealthy state, blocking all HTTPS traffic.
  Billing application itself is healthy on port 8443 internally.
observations.check_gateway_status:
KONG GATEWAY STATUS:
  Instance: kong-gateway-01
  Process:  RUNNING (PID 9341)
  Health:   UNHEALTHY
  Error:    SSL certificate expired for billing.company.com
  Upstreams: billing-upstream — UNREACHABLE (SSL error)
  Routes:   /billing/* — SSL termination FAILED
  Admin API: http://localhost:8001 — responding normally

  Action needed: Renew cert, deploy to Kong, restart gateway.
observations.renew_ssl_certificate:
SSL CERTIFICATE RENEWAL:
  Command: /opt/certbot/renew.sh --domain billing.company.com --force
  Status: SUCCESS

  New Certificate Details:
    Subject:     CN=billing.company.com
    Issuer:      Let's Encrypt Authority X3
    Valid From:  2024-02-03 11:43:00 UTC
    Valid Until: 2024-05-03 11:43:00 UTC
    Saved to:    /etc/letsencrypt/live/billing.company.com/

  NOTE: Certificate renewed but not yet deployed. Run deploy_new_certificate next.
observations.deploy_new_certificate:
CERTIFICATE DEPLOYMENT:
  Loading new cert into Kong gateway...
  Command: kong cert update --domain billing.company.com --cert /etc/letsencrypt/live/billing.company.com/fullchain.pem --key /etc/letsencrypt/live/billing.company.com/privkey.pem
  Status: SUCCESS
  New certificate loaded into Kong config.
  NOTE: Gateway restart required to activate the new certificate.
observations.restart_gateway:
KONG GATEWAY RESTART:
  Command: systemctl restart kong
  Status: SUCCESS
  Kong restarted in 3.1s
  Health check: HEALTHY
  SSL termination: ACTIVE with new certificate
  Upstream billing-service: REACHABLE
  Routes: /billing/* — responding with HTTP 200
observations.verify_ssl_handshake:
SSL HANDSHAKE VERIFICATION:
  Host: billing.company.com:443
  TLS Version: TLS 1.3
  Certificate: CN=billing.company.com (Let's Encrypt)
  Valid Until: 2024-05-03 11:43:00 UTC
  Chain: VALID
  Handshake: SUCCESS (42ms)
  No certificate errors detected.
observations.verify_billing_access:
BILLING SERVICE VERIFICATION:
  External URL: https://billing.company.com
  HTTP Status: 200 OK
  Response time: 187ms
  SSL: VALID
  Payment API endpoint: /api/v1/payments — HTTP 200
  Health endpoint: /health — {"status": "ok", "db": "connected"}
  Service fully operational.
observations.resolve_ticket:
TICKET-3182 RESOLVED:
  Root Cause: SSL certificate for billing.company.com expired at 11:00 UTC on 2024-02-03. Certificate auto-renewal was disabled, causing Let's Encrypt cert to expire without replacement. Expired cert caused Kong API gateway to enter unhealthy state, dropping all HTTPS connections.

  Actions Taken:
    1. Identified expired SSL cert via logs and cert inspection
    2. Identified unhealthy Kong gateway state
    3. Renewed SSL cert via certbot (/opt/certbot/renew.sh)
    4. Deployed new cert to Kong gateway
    5. Restarted Kong to activate new cert
    6. Verified HTTPS access and payment API

  Total Downtime: ~44 minutes
  Immediate Fix: Re-enable auto-renewal in certbot
  RCA Status: Complete — sent to CTO
  Status: Closed
observations.unlock_account: INFO: No account lock issue detected. All user accounts are in normal state. This is not an authentication problem — check SSL and gateway instead.
observations.reboot_server: CRITICAL: Full server reboot initiated. This extended the outage by an additional 4 minutes and did not resolve the SSL certificate issue. The root cause is an expired certificate, not a hardware or OS issue.
observations.clear_disk_space: INFO: Disk usage is at 52% — no disk space issue. This action was irrelevant to the billing service SSL outage.
observations.reset_password: INFO: No authentication issue detected. Password reset did not address the SSL certificate expiry causing the service outage.
observations.restart_vpn_gateway: INFO: VPN gateway is unrelated to the billing service SSL issue. This action had no effect.
